item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So because it’s a “psionic capacitor” it’s sucks any psychic energy around it into itself, including consciousness. Is that what happened to those monkeys, they had their minds sucked out and destroyed, leave nothing behind but non-sentient bodies?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow. Well, again, good job guessing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: moar plz

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Still here, just deep lurkin'. As evidenced by me responding to a comment from nearly 4 years ago.

item.title: Congrats!
item.content:encoded: Huh. Can't believe I haven't upvoted on this before. But anyways, congratulations on reaching the 100th upvote milestone for this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Precisely; i knew that this was about 1500, and was simply using "ghost" as a metaphor of Zachary's disembodied conscience.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He didn't mean literal ghosts, he more meant that Zachary's consciousness has been freed from his body and is now wandering through our minds and planting itself there, thus causing the effect where we believe he was a friend- kind of like a ghost.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Uh… no. This is an origin story for SCP-1500. No ghosts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
so, lemme get this straight; Zackary Callahan was a Promethean scientist, when the grid got shut down, and he got locked in a room with telekill

He was overexposed to the anti-memetic, which caused his mind to mutate from the radiation.

His mind also was stripped from his body, but his conscience was able to project itself into the world, or something, so now, his conscience is out of his body, or something, and it's trying to convince people that he's still there, even though he's not?

So, Zach is a ghost, whom is trying to convince people that his body is still alive.

Man, things are way more confusing when you think about them too much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh… it's a little goofy, especially at the end, and I don't buy it as a 1500 origin story, but… I actually think it works well enough on its own merits to get an upvote anyway. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love it.
It reminds me a of a semi-recurring villain from JLA:U or something. Very Twilight Zone-y too. Brilliant work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Per Voct's request, I've removed .org from the end of the email addresses to avoid any complications in the future if that web site were actually made.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It took me a couple of times to actually get it, but I absolutely love it. The way it's written, the concept, the character, it's brilliant. +1 with all my heart.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Voct, you appear to have upvoted twice. Though I appreciate the enthusiasm, I don't think you meant to do that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To clarify, Zach is an animate chunk of Telekill, powered by Zach's mnemonically indigestible soul. The reason why it can do what it can is because Telekill is basically a psionic capacitor, and 868 is keeping it perpetually topped off. The net result of this is that 1500 is brimming with psychic energy. However, because the animating consciousness is naturally just a human one, it doesn't really know how to use it, and has basically figured out only one trick so far.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I skimmed this the first time through, read the ending, hit the final line and went, "Wait, what?" Then I came here, saw the first comment and went back to read it again.

But I kind of still don't get it. I'm not sure what's being suggested here, or how it pertains to 1500's primary effect. If anything, I'm kind of disappointed now that it's been explained. Yeah, that's probably my main problem with this, which is stupid, because I'm also pretty sure I clamored for just this thing when 1500 was released.

At any rate, I really liked this line: "Between lingering Faerie glamours, Saljab mind-tricks, and Enforcers".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a neat little coincidence that 1500 is presented as a man made of plant, and this origin is mechanically reminiscent of the one in Alan Moore's Swamp Thing stories.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nah.

EDIT: And yeah, this version is much improved. Solid characterization on Zachary — he seems like a real d-bag. I can definitely see him trying to control world leaders.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, thank you very much for your help with the draft. This fixes most of the problems?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I'd back that.

Glad to see the story's finally up!

EDIT: And you did it while I was posting, cool bananas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do you think it would be a good idea to make the last line a link to SCP-1500?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eeeee! Yay!

Might suggest capitalizing the "gray" in the title. Makes it look nicer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like 1500 way more because of this story. The realization hit me like a ton of bricks, and the idea is really something that's going to stick with me for a while.

Really makes me think about all of those SCPs that, after the new telekill edit, were left with telekill usage, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At long last, the origin story for SCP-1500. By popular demand.

I'd like to also make note of the (apparently departed from the site) Ralphomon, who is to my knowledge the only person to correctly guess what SCP-1500 was made of.
